name: getPropertyList
description: Returns a list of all registered StelProperty IDs. Each ID follows the format 'ModuleName.propertyName' for properties automatically registered from StelModule objects, or a custom ID for manually registered properties. This is the primary function for discovering what properties are available in the current Stellarium session.
returnDescription: List of all registered property ID strings

compatibility.notes: Works on all Stellarium versions with StelProperty system (introduced in 0.15)

name: getAllProperties
description: Returns a list of pointers to all registered StelProperty objects. This provides direct access to each property's methods (getId, getValue, setValue, isReadOnly, etc.) for more detailed property manipulation.
returnDescription: List of all registered StelProperty object pointers

compatibility.notes: Works on all Stellarium versions with StelProperty system

name: getProperty
description: Returns a pointer to the StelProperty object with the specified ID. If the property is not found, a warning is logged unless noWarning is set to true. Use this to get direct access to a specific property's methods for reading, writing, or monitoring changes.

parameters.name: noWarning
parameters.description: If true, suppresses the warning message when property is not found (useful for plugins that may not be loaded)

returnDescription: Pointer to the StelProperty object, or null if not found

compatibility.notes: Works on all Stellarium versions with StelProperty system

name: getStelPropertyValue
description: Convenience method that directly returns the current value of a StelProperty without needing to first retrieve the StelProperty object. This is the most commonly used method for reading property values in scripts. Returns an invalid QVariant if the property is not found.

parameters.name: noWarning
parameters.description: If true, suppresses warning when property not found

returnDescription: The current value of the property as a QVariant, or invalid QVariant if not found

compatibility.notes: Works on all Stellarium versions with StelProperty system

name: setStelPropertyValue
signature: bool setStelPropertyValue(QString id, QVariant value, bool noWarning=false)
description: Convenience method that directly sets the value of a StelProperty without needing to first retrieve the StelProperty object. This is the most commonly used method for modifying property values in scripts. Returns false if the property is read-only, the value type is incompatible, or the property is not found. Data type conversion is performed automatically using QVariant logic when possible.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The new value to set (automatically converted to the property's type if compatible)

parameters.name: noWarning
parameters.description: If true, suppresses warning when property not found or write fails

returnDescription: true if the value was successfully changed, false if the property was not found, is read-only, or value is incompatible

compatibility.notes: Works on all Stellarium versions with StelProperty system

name: getMetaProperty
description: Returns the QMetaProperty metadata for a given property ID. This contains compile-time information about the property including its type, read/write methods, notify signal, and other Qt property system attributes. Primarily useful for advanced introspection and debugging.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique property ID to get metadata for

returnDescription: QMetaProperty object containing property metadata, or invalid QMetaProperty if not found

name: registerProperty
signature: StelProperty* registerProperty(QString id, QObject* target, char* propertyName)
description: Manually registers a single Q_PROPERTY as a StelProperty. The property ID must be unique (the application will exit if a duplicate is registered). The target object must have a Q_PROPERTY with the specified name. This is rarely needed for StelModules (they are auto-registered), but useful for custom objects or non-module properties that need to be exposed to the scripting system.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the property (must follow naming conventions, e.g., 'MyModule.myProperty')

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The QObject instance that contains the property

parameters.name: propertyName
parameters.description: The name of the Q_PROPERTY on the target object to register

returnDescription: Pointer to the newly created StelProperty object

compatibility.notes: Application exits if duplicate ID is registered. StelModules auto-register all Q_PROPERTY definitions - manual registration is typically done in C++ code.

name: registerObject
description: Automatically registers all Q_PROPERTY definitions of a QObject as StelProperty instances. Each property is registered with an ID in the format '<objectName>.<propertyName>'. The object must have a non-empty QObject::objectName without dots. All StelModules are automatically registered by the StelModuleMgr when they are loaded, so manual registration is typically not needed.

parameters.name: obj
parameters.description: The QObject whose Q_PROPERTY definitions should be registered

compatibility.notes: Object must have a valid, non-empty objectName without dots. Application exits if duplicate IDs are found.

name: getId
description: Returns the unique string identifier of this StelProperty. The ID follows the format '<objectName>.<propertyName>' for auto-registered StelModule properties, or a custom ID for manually registered properties. Use this to identify which property you are working with.

compatibility.notes: Called on a StelProperty instance, not on StelPropertyMgr

name: getValue
description: Returns the current value of this StelProperty as a QVariant. The actual data type depends on the Q_PROPERTY definition (e.g., bool, int, double, QString, or custom types). QVariant provides automatic type conversion for most standard data types.
returnDescription: The current property value as a QVariant

compatibility.notes: Called on a StelProperty instance. Must have a valid READ accessor or MEMBER defined.

name: setValue
description: Sets a new value for this StelProperty. Returns false if the property is read-only (no WRITE accessor or non-writable MEMBER) or if the value type is incompatible. Data type conversion is attempted automatically using QVariant::canConvert logic. The value will not be written if it is identical to the current value (optimization to prevent unnecessary events).

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The new value to set (will be automatically type-converted if compatible)

returnDescription: true if the value was successfully set, false if read-only or incompatible type

compatibility.notes: Called on a StelProperty instance. Requires WRITE accessor or writable MEMBER.

name: isReadOnly
description: Returns whether this property is read-only. A property is read-only when it has no WRITE accessor and is not a writable MEMBER. Read-only properties cannot be modified through setValue() or setStelPropertyValue(). Examples of read-only properties include the application version string and computed values.
returnDescription: true if the property is read-only (cannot be modified), false if writable

compatibility.notes: Reflects the Qt property system's isWritable() status

name: isSynchronizable
description: Returns whether this property can be synchronized with external sources (such as the RemoteControl plugin). A property is synchronizable when it is both writable AND marked as STORED (which is the default for Q_PROPERTY). Non-stored properties are typically computed or transient values that should not be persisted or synchronized.
returnDescription: true if the property can be synchronized externally

compatibility.notes: Used by RemoteControl and similar plugins for state synchronization

name: canNotify
description: Returns whether this property has a NOTIFY signal defined. Properties with NOTIFY signals emit the 'changed' signal whenever their value changes, allowing other components to react to changes in real-time. If false, you must poll the property to detect changes.
returnDescription: true if the property has a NOTIFY signal and can emit change notifications

compatibility.notes: Reflects whether hasNotifySignal() is true for the Q_PROPERTY

name: getType
description: Returns the data type of this property as a QMetaType::Type enum value. This tells you the underlying C++ type (e.g., QMetaType::Bool, QMetaType::Int, QMetaType::Double, QMetaType::QString). Useful for type checking before setting values or for generating type-appropriate UI controls.
returnDescription: The QMetaType enum value representing the property's data type

compatibility.notes: Uses Qt version-specific type ID handling for Qt5/Qt6 compatibility

name: getMetaProp
description: Returns the QMetaProperty object wrapped by this StelProperty. This provides access to the full Qt property system metadata, including the property name, type name, read/write method signatures, notify signal signature, and other attributes. Primarily used for debugging, introspection, and advanced type handling.
returnDescription: The QMetaProperty object containing full property metadata

compatibility.notes: Provides direct access to Qt's property system metadata

name: getTarget
description: Returns a pointer to the QObject instance that owns this property. This is the object where the Q_PROPERTY was defined. For auto-registered StelModule properties, this is the module instance itself (e.g., MilkyWay, ConstellationMgr, LandscapeMgr). Useful for understanding which component a property belongs to.
returnDescription: Pointer to the QObject that owns this property

compatibility.notes: The target object is set during registration and never changes
